reportingEsg.title: ESG and Corporate Reporting
reportingEsg.body: We align TSRS, CSRD/ESRS, GRI and ISSB work into one reporting-ready structure: double materiality, disclosure topics, ESG data and evidence ownership. We prepare sustainability reporting packs that stand up to review and stakeholder questions.

sustainableFinance.title: Market and Stakeholder Readiness
sustainableFinance.body: We prepare buyer and tender responses, ESG maturity views, investor-facing materials and stakeholder messaging with consistent evidence. We help market-facing claims rest on a credible file structure.

certificationTraceability.title: Certification, Traceability and Market Confidence
certificationTraceability.body: We support ASI preparation, certification readiness, chain of custody, mass balance and pre-audit evidence files with clear roles and records. We connect market claims to traceable proof.

hero.title: Advisory for CSRD, ESRS and corporate ESG reporting
hero.text: ANKA works with your ESG data architecture, reporting boundaries and evidence design alongside CSRD, ESRS, GRI, ISSB and TSRS expectations. We tighten the full line—from double materiality through disclosure topics to data ownership—into one workable operating rhythm. The aim is to move reporting beyond a periodic document and embed a trackable, defensible structure that connects to decision-making. Before each reporting cycle we review indicators, evidence and internal controls, and align stakeholder and finance perspectives in the same file logic.

positioning.title: European perspective with practical delivery maturity
positioning.text: Through a Netherlands-anchored company structure and strong Türkiye operations, ANKA works with European market expectations alongside the realities of industrial implementation. The aim is to make regulation, data, evidence, and compliance more manageable for manufacturers, suppliers, and exporters.

c1.title: European market perspective
c1.body: We translate EU expectations and buyer data needs into a structured technical work scope.

c2.title: Türkiye operations strength
c2.body: We stay close to production, sourcing, and export environments where decisions and constraints are formed.

c3.title: Implementable consulting model
c3.body: We bring regulatory understanding together with data, evidence, calculations and workflow—then translate it into something teams can actually run.

founderPerspective.title: Clear, Practical And Trustworthy Advisory
founderPerspective.body: Under the leadership of Burcu Şimşek, ANKA does not treat sustainability and compliance work as a reporting or documentation exercise only. In each project we start by clarifying the organisation’s real needs, data structure and decision-making context together—then translate technical requirements into a practical way of working.
founderPerspective.body2: What matters most to us is that organisations can see what must be done, why it matters and how it can be sustained internally. We do not stop at explaining regulatory expectations; we organise data, evidence, responsibilities and process steps so the work becomes usable inside the organisation.
founderPerspective.body3: Nearly 20 years across sustainability, carbon management, verification, LCA, EPD and EU compliance processes makes it natural to keep technical rigour and operational reality in the same conversation.
founderPerspective.name: Burcu Şimşek
founderPerspective.quote:
At ANKA we do not want to stop at delivering a report or a compliance file. We want organisations to see sustainability and compliance topics more clearly, anchor decisions in data and keep the momentum inside the organisation.

That is why technical accuracy and practical usability carry equal weight for us. We tie regulation, data and evidence structures to day-to-day operations—so outcomes are more reliable, defensible and genuinely useful.

hero.description: Selected guidance on regulatory readiness, product data, carbon, supply chain, and digital implementation—designed to be practical and reusable.

csrdMateriality.title: Where To Begin With CSRD And Double Materiality Readiness
csrdMateriality.description: Foundational preparation steps to start corporate reporting in a more robust, traceable and manageable way.
csrdMateriality.body:
CSRD readiness is not only about producing a report. If you don’t clarify what is material, which boundaries apply, and what evidence will support decisions, the process becomes unmanageable.

A strong starting frame:
- Clarify scope and expectations (ESRS, stakeholders, boundaries)
- Treat double materiality as a decision logic—not a checklist
- Design data ownership and evidence flow early

The common failure mode is running analysis and data/documentation as separate tracks. ANKA’s approach ties materiality logic to a workable data structure and delivery model.

cbamCost.title: How To Structure Data And Financial Impact Readiness For CBAM
cbamCost.description: A controlled preparation frame for reporting and for financial logic, data quality and supplier information—not disconnected spreadsheets.
cbamCost.body:
CBAM preparation has two layers: (1) data and methodology discipline for embedded emissions, and (2) visibility that translates exposure into management language.

To structure it well:
- Standardize product/installation splits and methodology choices
- Manage supplier submissions with format, quality, and traceability controls
- Make default-value boundaries explicit and governed
- Link emissions outputs to financial impact through scenarios

The goal is not “last-minute data chasing” during reporting windows, but a controlled, repeatable calculation and decision-support flow.

dppPrep.title: Critical Data Fields In Digital Product Passport Readiness
dppPrep.description: Key record, traceability and declaration areas to solidify when managing product data at the right level of depth.
dppPrep.body:
Digital Product Passport (DPP) readiness requires treating product data not as a file, but as a governed record system with relationships and freshness controls.

Critical data areas:
- Product identity and variant/SKU logic
- Material and component structure (BOM) and sourcing information
- Compliance and declaration data (regulation, standard, verification)
- Traceability and supplier records

Success is not only listing fields—it’s building ownership, evidence links, and sharing scenarios as an executable workflow.

ppwrProductData.title: How Product And Packaging Data Expectations Are Changing With PPWR
ppwrProductData.description: Why product and packaging information in European markets is moving toward measurable, evidence-led disclosure.
ppwrProductData.body:
With PPWR, packaging becomes more than design or cost—it becomes a data, declaration, and compliance obligation.

Practical implications:
- Linking packaging composition and material data to the product level
- Translating obligations into concrete data fields
- Making fee/charge logic visible and traceable

That’s why organizations need a structure that manages packaging inventory together with compliance logic and decision-support outputs.

scope3Suppliers.title: What Structure Should You Use When Collecting Scope 3 Supplier Data
scope3Suppliers.description: Practical design patterns to make fragmented data flows more ordered, traceable and manageable over time.
scope3Suppliers.body:
Scope 3 is often handled as a one-off questionnaire. The real value comes from building a durable data flow.

For an operational structure:
- Supplier segmentation and prioritization
- Field dictionary (definitions), format standards, and quality controls
- Document/evidence flow and closure discipline

The target is not just reporting—it’s making supply chain data traceable, manageable, and reusable.

reportingWorkflow.title: Why A Digital Working Space Matters In Sustainability Reporting
reportingWorkflow.description: The difference a structured workspace can make to progress, visibility, evidence logic and quality of delivery.
reportingWorkflow.body:
Reporting projects fall apart when data ownership, revision control, evidence links, and progress tracking are weak.

A digital workspace strengthens:
- Data collection and responsibility tracking
- Control steps and a traceable audit trail
- Repeatable reporting flows and quality standards

Digital tools are not meant to replace consulting—they should make the delivery model more controlled and defensible.

intellectualProperty.body: Text, layout, graphics, and branding elements are owned by ANKA or used under appropriate licence. Copying or redistributing content for commercial purposes without prior written consent is not permitted.

limitation.title: Limitation of reliance
limitation.body: Information on this website does not constitute legal, financial, or technical advice tailored to your organisation. Decisions should be based on professional judgement and, where appropriate, specialist counsel.

contact.title: Contact
contact.body: Questions about these terms may be directed to info@ankasustainability.com.

meta.title: Legal Notice
meta.description: Operator and legal information for the ANKA Sustainability Consulting B.V. website.

legalNoticePage.intro: This website is operated by ANKA Sustainability Consulting B.V. It presents our sustainability consultancy services and software-supported delivery capabilities in a consultancy-led model.
legalNoticePage.statement: ANKA Sustainability Consulting B.V. provides professional sustainability consultancy services. Software and digital tools are described as enabling capabilities within that consultancy relationship, not as a separate consumer product brand.

sidebar.foundationNote: This workspace is PPWR-first and designed to extend into ESPR / DPP modules without refactoring the core architecture.

overview.title: Overview
overview.subtitle: A regulation-native operational workspace for packaging compliance, evidence, responsibility mapping, and packaging cost decisions.

tables.risksSubtitle: Findings are structured outputs of the rule framework. PPWR logic will be added iteratively.
tables.scenariosSubtitle: Compare baseline vs alternative packaging and capture decision notes.

products.title: Products & SKUs
products.subtitle: Maintain product master data and link SKUs to packaging units, markets, evidence and technical file readiness.

table.title: SKU list
table.subtitle: Search, filter and open SKUs. Import/export hooks are reserved for the next package.

bom.title: Packaging BOM
bom.subtitle: Build packaging units and component structures with material and supplier links. Designed for PPWR-first checks and later DPP readiness.

table.title: BOM components
table.subtitle: A component-level view across packaging units (primary/secondary/tertiary/transport).

responsibility.title: Responsibility Mapping
responsibility.subtitle: Map economic operator responsibilities by market and supply chain context (manufacturer/importer/private label/retailer).

operator.title: Operator roles
operator.description: Define responsibility templates and apply them to SKUs and markets with traceable assumptions.

market.title: Market overlays
market.description: Attach country overlays for national nuances without hard-coding them into product pages.

audit.title: Audit-ready mapping
audit.description: Prepare exportable, customer/importer-ready responsibility output packs.

table.title: Mapping register (foundation)
table.subtitle: This foundation provides the structure; operator rules and approvals come in the next packages.

risks.title: Compliance Risks
risks.subtitle: Run structured rule checks and triage findings across markets, SKUs, packaging units, and evidence.

table.title: Findings
table.subtitle: Severity and category are rule-driven; effective-date and country overlays are first-class in the architecture.

evidence.title: Evidence Pack
evidence.subtitle: Collect supplier evidence, track review/approval state, and prepare audit-ready documentation packs.

empty.title: No evidence items yet
empty.description: Start by defining your evidence requirements and sending supplier requests. Status and workflow are built-in to support review and approval.

table.title: Evidence register
table.subtitle: Request → Received → Review → Approved flow with missing/expired handling.

scenarios.title: Scenario Comparison
scenarios.subtitle: Compare baseline packaging vs alternatives: weight reduction, material changes, compliance delta, documentation delta, and decision notes.

empty.title: No scenarios yet
empty.description: Create a scenario to compare current vs alternative packaging and capture decisions with traceable notes.

table.title: Scenarios
table.subtitle: Weight delta is computed today; compliance/documentation delta slots are wired for PPWR rule output.

technicalFile.title: Technical File
technicalFile.subtitle: Track technical documentation readiness per SKU. Evidence and rule outputs contribute to a readiness view.

table.title: Readiness by SKU
table.subtitle: Readiness is evidence-driven in this foundation; rule-driven deltas will follow.

suppliers.title: Suppliers
suppliers.subtitle: Manage suppliers, onboarding status, evidence SLA expectations, and document collection performance.

table.title: Supplier register
table.subtitle: Evidence status is linked to suppliers to support accountability and supplier portal expansion later.

markets.title: Markets
markets.subtitle: Maintain market scope and reserve explicit overlay slots for national administrative nuances and EPR variations.

table.title: Market scope
table.subtitle: Overlays attach per market without entangling the core PPWR structure.

settings.title: Settings
settings.subtitle: Workspace configuration: roles, permissions, markets, rule catalogs, and effective dates.

roles.title: Roles & permissions
roles.description: Define who can manage products, BOM, evidence and technical file exports.

markets.title: Market scope
markets.description: Activate markets and attach overlay notes for local nuances.

catalogs.title: Rule catalogs
catalogs.description: PPWR-first catalogs live here. ESPR/DPP catalogs can be added later without changing the product UI structure.

effectiveDates.title: Effective dates
effectiveDates.description: Model future effective dates and transitional arrangements as first-class inputs to rule evaluation.
